title: 10 best herbal capsules for shingles
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for shingles are a natural remedy used to alleviate symptoms associated with this painful viral infection. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and garlic, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce pain, itching, and inflammation caused by shingles. For example, Capsicum annuum (hot pepper) capsules can help relieve nerve pain, while Valerian root capsules can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. By using these herbal capsules, individuals with shingles can experience improved symptoms management, reduced discomfort, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to resume their normal daily activities and improve their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with shingles because of their potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a hallmark symptom of shingles. Additionally, curcumin has antiviral properties that can help combat the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. By reducing inflammation and targeting the virus directly, turmeric capsules may help alleviate symptoms such as pain, itching, and rash associated with shingles, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with shingles.

Antiviral property: Turmeric's antiviral properties can help combat the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) responsible for causing shingles, potentially shortening the duration and severity of the outbreak.
Analgesic property: Curcumin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-hyperalgesic effects, which means it can help reduce pain and sensitivity caused by shingles, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in curcuminoids, a group of compounds responsible for many of turmeric's therapeutic properties, which can help alleviate shingles symptoms such as pain and inflammation.

Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain high concentrations of volatile oils, including beta-caryophyllene, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce shingles-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried turmeric root curcuma longa.
Grind 50g of the harvested turmeric into fine powder using coffee grinder.
Mix 250ml of coconut oil with 5g of ground turmeric powder in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally to infuse.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and fill 20 capsules with 500mg each using capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles. Additionally, St John's Wort has been found to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. As a natural remedy, St John's Wort capsules may provide relief from the discomfort and itching associated with shingles, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This bioactive compound has anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and prevent the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes shingles.

Hyporhysoflavonoids: These flavonoid compounds have antioxidant properties that can help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by shingles, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting wound healing.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort capsules have anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of VZV, thereby shortening the duration and severity of shingles symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, the roots provide a concentrated source of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in soothing and calming the skin during shingles outbreaks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers and dry them in a warm place for 24 hours.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, taking about 5 minutes to achieve desired texture.
Mix one part of the powdered hypericum perforatum with two parts of tapioca starch and fill size 00 capsules to yield approximately 250-300 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage, taking out as needed.
Take three capsules twice daily or as directed by a healthcare professional, allowing for at least 2 hours between doses and monitoring progress over several weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with shingles because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with this viral infection. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby alleviating symptoms like itching, burning, and numbness. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties may help boost the immune system, allowing it to fight off the underlying virus that causes shingles more effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles rash and blisters.

Antiviral properties: Ginger has been shown to have antiviral effects against the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes shingles, potentially helping to slow or prevent viral replication and transmission.
Pain-relieving properties: Ginger's analgesic compounds can help alleviate nerve pain and discomfort associated with shingles, making it a natural complement to conventional treatments.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of ginger have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with shingles.
Roots: Ginger roots have antiviral properties, which can help combat the herpes virus that causes shingles and alleviate its symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root and wash it thoroughly under cold running water.
Slice the washed ginger into thin pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven for two hours.
Combine one part dried ginger slices with four parts carrier powder such as potato starch or silica gel in a bowl.
Add 500 milligrams of dried ginger to each capsule shell and seal tightly before filling remaining shells with mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with shingles because they contain a natural adaptogen that helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for shingle outbreaks. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help to alleviate the painful rashes and blisters associated with shingles. Additionally, ashwagandha's antioxidant activity may help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of the condition. By promoting overall well-being and calming the body's response to stress, ashwagandha capsules can provide relief from shingle symptoms and support a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with shingles, while its anti-inflammatory effects can alleviate pain, itching, and discomfort.

Immune-modulating activity: Ashwagandha has been shown to enhance immune function, which can help the body fight off the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Antioxidant and neuroprotective effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in nerve tissues, which may help alleviate shingles-related neuralgia (nerve pain) and prevent long-term damage to nerves.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ashwagandha are rich in bioactive compounds like withanolides and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in treating shingles symptoms.

Seeds: Ashwagandha seeds contain a significant amount of saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Dry one hundred grams of withania somnifera roots in the shade for two hours using a food dehydrator at 50°c temperature.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for three minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Sift the powdered root through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles retaining one hundred and fifty grams of sifted powder.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the sifted withania somnifera powder using an automatic capsule filler at room temperature.

Store the filled capsules in airtight glass containers in the refrigerator for up to six months or freeze them for longer preservation ensuring proper labeling and handling procedures are followed throughout.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with shingles because they contain potent herbs that soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with this painful condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbal teas help to alleviate swelling and redness, promoting faster healing and relief from itching and burning sensations. Additionally, some herbal teas have antiviral properties that can help combat the underlying virus causing shingles, shortening the duration of the outbreak and reducing the risk of future occurrences.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas containing anti-inflammatory compounds, such as turmeric or ginger, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate the pain associated with shingles.

Antiviral properties: Herbal teas with antiviral properties, such as echinacea or elderberry, can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Pain-relieving properties: Herbal teas containing pain-relieving compounds, such as willow bark or valerian root, can help alleviate the severe pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks of the tea plant contain flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Measure dried camellia sinensis leaves at 2000mg and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine powdered camellia sinensis with vitamin c powder in a ratio of 3:1 and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency and shelf life.
Label and date the filled capsules, storing them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Consume 2-3 capsules twice daily for 10 days, then reduce to 1-2 capsules twice daily for maintenance treatment as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cat's claw capsules helps with shingles because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These active ingredients, such as alkaloids and flavonoids, work to reduce pain, inflammation, and itching associated with shingles, making them an effective natural remedy for managing symptoms. Additionally, the capsules' antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary infections, promoting a faster recovery from this uncomfortable and often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Cat's claw contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes viruses, including those responsible for shingles, which can help reduce the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in cat's claw capsules can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with shingles rash and blisters, which can alleviate discomfort and promote healing.
Immune system support: Cat's claw has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, which can help prevent future shingles outbreaks by supporting the body's natural defenses against viruses.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems store nutrients and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Roots: Rich in alkaloids, roots of the cat's claw plant have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including shingles, by reducing itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried uncaria tomentosa herb and measure out 20 grams for the batch.
Combine the measured herb with 40 grams of organic silica gel in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 24 hours to allow the herb to infuse into the silica gel.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the mixture into a fine powder, taking about 15 minutes to complete.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the powdered herbal blend and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with shingles because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound reduces the inflammation and pain associated with shingles, allowing for faster healing and reduced discomfort. Additionally, licorice root has antiviral properties that help combat the virus responsible for causing shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. By incorporating herbal licorice capsules into your treatment plan, you can alleviate symptoms and promote a speedier recovery from this painful condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with shingles.
Antiviral property: Herbal licorice contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus, the virus responsible for causing shingles.

Immune system modulating property: Licorice root has been found to enhance immune function and increase the production of white blood cells, which can help fight off the viral infection and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating the pain and itching associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix one part powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root with two parts empty gelatin capsules to create 200 capsules.
Fill each capsule shell with 250 milligrams of the glycyrrhiza glabra powder and seal immediately.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock capsules helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The plant-based extracts in burdock capsules, such as arctiin and lappa, have been shown to reduce pain, inflammation, and itching associated with shingles. Additionally, burdock's antiviral compounds may help suppress the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. As a natural remedy, burdock capsules offer a safe and effective way to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery from shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Burdock's antiviral compounds, such as arctiin and lignans, can help inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes shingles.

Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles, as well as prevent post-herpetic neuralgia (PHN).
Immune-modulating property: Burdock's immunomodulatory effects can help stimulate the immune system to fight off the VZV infection, reducing the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that soothes and calms irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for shingle-related itchiness and inflammation.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of fatty acids, antioxidants, and polyphenols, burdock seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried arctium lappa roots and leaves measuring 500 grams.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 500 grams of silica gel in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a total of 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture per capsule preparation time one hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with shingles because they contain a potent blend of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated nerve endings, which is particularly effective in relieving the intense itching and burning sensations associated with shingles. Additionally, the capsules' antioxidant properties help boost the immune system, supporting the body's natural defense against the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. This holistic approach can provide relief from shingles symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Ginkgo biloba's antiviral compounds, such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, may help inhibit the replication of the herpes virus that causes shingles, reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain, inflammation, and discomfort associated with shingles rash and blisters.

Neuroprotective properties: Ginkgo biloba's neuroprotective compounds may help protect against nerve damage caused by the herpes virus, reducing the risk of post-herpetic neuralgia (PHN) and other long-term complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with shingles.
Barks: Contain antiviral properties that inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Crush the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered ginkgo biloba with two parts capsule filler material such as potato starch or silica dioxide.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with shingles because they contain a natural compound called valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the painful symptoms of shingles. The herb also has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can aid in relaxing and calming the body, reducing stress and anxiety that often exacerbates the condition. Additionally, valerian's antispasmodic properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and itching associated with shingles, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with shingles, allowing for better sleep and reduced inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Valerian root contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can exacerbate shingle symptoms.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Valerian root has been traditionally used to alleviate pain, including nerve-related pain associated with shingles, by interacting with opioid receptors in the brain and spinal cord.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valepotriates, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis root material in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested valeriana officinalis root material using air circulation for 24 hours.
Grind 500g of cleaned valeriana officinalis root material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground valeriana officinalis with 2 parts silica filler in a bowl to create the capsule formula.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the prepared valeriana officinalis-silica mixture and seal tightly.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shingles with herbal capsules is to combine them with a healthy lifestyle. Ashwagandha capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and discomfort. St. John's Wort capsules have antiviral properties that can help combat the virus causing shingles. Additionally, Echinacea capsules can boost the immune system and reduce the severity of symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shingles that are treated with herbal capsules are herpes zoster, postherpetic neuralgia (PHN), and acute viral ganglionitis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, and passionflower may help alleviate symptoms like pain, itching, and burning sensations associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbs like St. John's Wort and lavender have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce discomfort.
